technology.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
technology.description: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist) is a GA family with 40 normalized items, 9 high-severity findings, covers Network Topology and Connectivity, and touches services like Azure Application Gateway, Azure Front Door, Azure Load Balancer, Azure Traffic Manager.
technology.whatThisMeans: Suitable for executive and architecture review packs by default.

quality.label: GA · High confidence
quality.summary: Strong review accelerator. Quality score 93 with 83% metadata completeness and 100% severity coverage.

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Perform app delivery within landing zones for both internal-facing (corp) and external-facing apps (online).

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Ensure you are using Application Gateway v2 SKU
items.graph: resources | where type == 'microsoft.network/applicationgateways' | project id, compliant = properties.sku.name in ('Standard_v2', 'WAF_v2') | project id,compliant

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Ensure you are using the Standard SKU for your Azure Load Balancers
items.graph: resources | where type == 'microsoft.network/loadbalancers' | project id, compliant=(tolower(sku.name) == 'standard')

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Ensure your Load Balancers frontend IP addresses are zone-redundant (unless you require zonal frontends).

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Your Application Gateways v2 should be deployed in subnets with IP prefixes equal or larger than /24
items.graph: resources | where type=='microsoft.network/applicationgateways' | extend subnetId = tostring(properties.gatewayIPConfigurations[0].properties.subnet.id) | project id, subnetId | join (resources | where type=='microsoft.network/virtualnetworks' | project id,subnets=properties.subnets | mv-expand subnets | mv-expand subnets.properties.addressPrefixes | project id, subnetId = tostring(subnets.id), prefix1 = subnets.properties.addressPrefix, prefix2 = subnets.properties.addressPrefixes | mv-expand prefix2 | extend prefix = iff(isnotnull(prefix1), prefix1, prefix2) | extend subnetPrefixLength = split(prefix, '/')[1])on subnetId | extend compliant = (subnetPrefixLength <= 24 or subnetPrefixLength == 64) | distinct id,compliant

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Deploy Azure Application Gateway v2 or partner NVAs used for proxying inbound HTTP(S) connections within the landing-zone virtual network and with the apps that they're securing.
items.description: Administration of reverse proxies in general and WAF in particular is closer to the application than to networking, so they belong in the same subscription as the app. Centralizing the Application Gateway and WAF in the connectivity subscription might be OK if it is managed by one single team.

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Use a DDoS Network or IP protection plans for all Public IP addresses in application landing zones.

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Configure autoscaling with a minimum amount of instances of two.
items.graph: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.network/applicationGateways' | extend compliant = (isnotnull(properties.autoscaleConfiguration) and properties.autoscaleConfiguration.minCapacity >= 2) | distinct id,compliant

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Deploy Application Gateway across Availability Zones
items.graph: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.network/applicationGateways' | extend compliant = (isnotnull(zones) and array_length(zones) > 1) | distinct id,compliant

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: When using Front Door and Application Gateway to help protect HTTP/S apps, use WAF policies in Front Door. Lock down Application Gateway to receive traffic only from Front Door.

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Use Traffic Manager to deliver global apps that span protocols other than HTTP/S.

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: If users only need access to internal applications, has Microsoft Entra ID Application Proxy been considered as an alternative to Azure Virtual Desktop (AVD)?

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: To reduce the number of firewall ports open for incoming connections in your network, consider using Microsoft Entra ID Application Proxy to give remote users secure and authenticated access to internal applications.

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Use Azure NAT Gateway instead of Load Balancer outbound rules for better SNAT scalability
items.graph: resources | where type=='microsoft.network/loadbalancers' | extend countOutRules=array_length(properties.outboundRules) | extend compliant = (countOutRules == 0) | distinct id,compliant

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Enable the Azure Application Gateway WAF bot protection rule set. The bot rules detect good and bad bots.
items.graph: resources | where type == 'microsoft.network/applicationgatewaywebapplicationfirewallpolicies' | mv-expand properties.managedRules.managedRuleSets | project id, rulesettype = properties_managedRules_managedRuleSets.ruleSetType | extend compliant1 = (rulesettype == 'Microsoft_BotManagerRuleSet') | project id, compliant1 | summarize compliant = max(compliant1) by id

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Ensure if request body inspection feature is enabled in Azure Application Gateway WAF policy.
items.graph: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.network/applicationgatewaywebapplicationfirewallpolicies' | extend compliant = (properties['policySettings']['requestBodyCheck'] == 'true' and properties['policySettings']['state'] =~ 'Enabled') | distinct id, name, compliant

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Tune the Azure Application Gateway WAF in detection mode for your workload. Reduce false positive detections.

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Deploy your WAF policy for Application Gateway in 'Prevention' mode.
items.graph: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.network/applicationgatewaywebapplicationfirewallpolicies' | extend compliant = (properties['policySettings']['mode'] =~ 'Prevention')| where properties['policySettings']['mode'] =~ 'Prevention' | distinct id, name, compliant

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Add rate limiting to the Azure Application Gateway WAF. Rate limiting blocks clients accidentally or intentionally sending large amounts of traffic in a short period of time.

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Use a high threshold for Azure Application Gateway WAF rate limits. High rate limit thresholds avoid blocking legitimate traffic, while still providing protection against extremely high numbers of requests that might overwhelm your infrastructure.

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: If you are not expecting traffic from all geographical regions, use geo-filters to block traffic from non-expected countries.

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Specify the unknown (ZZ) location when geo-filtering traffic with the Azure Application Gateway WAF. Avoid accidentally blocking legitimate requests when IP addresses can't be geo-matched.

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Use the latest Azure Application Gateway WAF rule set version. Rule set updates are regularly updated to take account of the current threat landscape.

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Add diagnostic settings to save your Azure Application Gateway WAF logs.

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Send Azure Application Gateway WAF logs to Microsoft Sentinel.

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Define your Azure Application Gateway WAF configuration as code. By using code, you can more easily adopt new rule set version and gain additional protection.

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Use WAF Policies instead of the legacy WAF configuration.

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Filter inbound traffic in the backends so that they only accept connections from the Application Gateway subnet, for example with NSGs.

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: You should encrypt traffic to the backend servers.
items.graph: resources | where type == 'microsoft.network/applicationgateways'| extend compliant = (properties['backendHttpSettingsCollection'][0]['properties']['port'] =~ '443') |where properties['backendHttpSettingsCollection'][0]['properties']['port'] =~ '443'|distinct id,name,compliant

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: You should use a Web Application Firewall.

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Redirect HTTP to HTTPS

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Use gateway-managed cookies to direct traffic from a user session to the same server for processing

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Enable connection draining during planned service updates to prevent connection loss to existing members of the backend pool

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Create custom error pages to display a personalized user experience

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Edit HTTP requests and response headers for easier routing and information exchange between the client and server

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Configure Front Door to optimize global web traffic routing and top-tier end-user performance, and reliability through quick global failover

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Use transport layer load balancing

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Configure routing based on host or domain name for multiple web applications on a single gateway

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Centralize SSL certificate management to reduce encryption and decryption overhead from a backend server farm

items.technology: Azure Application Delivery Networking (Network Appdelivery Checklist)
items.text: Use Application Gateway for native support for WebSocket and HTTP/2 protocols
